Empires & Puzzles Rhineglow Review: 5-Star Nature Hero Guide

Rhineglow brings a physics lesson to the battlefield with a massive health boost, a short fuse Taunt, and a stored damage explosion. Is this green giant a tanking genius or just too slow for the modern meta? We break down the math behind the First Law of Thermodynamics.
Rhineglow Review: The Thermodynamics of Taunt
Rhineglow: The Indestructible Green Giant of Thermodynamics
Rhineglow is a 5-star Legendary Nature (Green) hero from the

. He represents a fascinating shift in tank design: a Slow speed hero who offers a very short, volatile window of protection (Taunt) combined with massive sustainability and a delayed nuclear payload. Unlike traditional "wall" tanks who sit there and absorb hits forever, Rhineglow is a ticking time bomb. He invites you to hit him, heals his team past their maximum HP, and then returns the favor with interest.
He fits the archetype of the
—if you can't dispel or kill him within 3 turns, your team is going to suffer major consequences.

Rhineglow is an absolute unit. He looks like a magical golem forged from enchanted stone and glowing emerald energy. The "Construct" aesthetic is strong here, with floating segments and a core that looks like it's about to go critical. He visually communicates "high defense" and "imminent explosion" perfectly. It’s a nice change of pace to see a monster that looks like it could actually bench press a castle, rather than another elf in spandex.
Rhineglow's stats are thoroughly modern and built for punishment.
Surprisingly high for a defensive hero, which helps his tile damage and the "Destructive Core" passive.
An absolute fortress. Breaking 1000 Defense raw is the new gold standard for tanks.
Massive. When you factor in emblems and Limit Breaks, he becomes a HP sponge of the highest order.
Compared to the latest heroes, Rhineglow sits comfortably in the top tier of bulk. He is designed to survive long enough to fire, even at Slow speed.
Special Skill: First Law of Thermodynamics
This skill is a complex engine of survival and retribution. Let's break it down:

This is the controversial part. For a Slow hero, 3 turns is short. Ludwig gives 6 turns. Krampus gives 3 (but is Average speed). However, this Taunt isn't just for protection; it's a lure to charge his "Stored Damage."
This is huge. 400 HP per turn is a massive heal, and because it's boosted, it overheals your team. Since it's
, even if the Taunt is stripped, the healing train keeps rolling.
He records the damage he takes. When the 3 turns end, he unleashes it on
, distributed to enemies (wait, clarification below in "By The Numbers").
The synergy here is tight. He taunts to force enemies to hit him. He stores that damage. He heals himself (and allies) to survive the damage he's forcing them to deal. Then he explodes.
Let's do the math on why this giant is dangerous.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Construct family is notoriously strong because their passives are essentially extra special skills.
The Construct Core mechanic rewards you for taking hits—which is exactly what a Taunt hero wants to do. It charges up purely by surviving.
Deals damage when he casts. 180% to all is a "Soft Hit" on its own, comparable to some Fast heroes.
Vital for a Slow hero. He can't be stopped by Miki, Peters, or Silence effects easily. This ensures that once he's charged, he
For a Slow hero, starting with Taunt protects your team from fast snipers immediately.
It buys him time to find those 12 tiles.
This makes him viable in standard wars, not just Rush.
Green tanks have often struggled (Yunan, Heimdall). Rhineglow offers a modern, aggressive take on the Green tank slot.

His stats are cutting edge. His heal (1200 fixed) is future-proofed against high HP pools better than low % heals, but worse than high % heals as HP climbs to 4000+. However, 1200 is still a massive chunk.

He is a monster in Rush Attack and a very strong contender in standard formations thanks to his Alpha Aether Auto-Taunt. He suffers slightly from the speed meta where battles end in 4 turns, but if he fires, he usually stabilizes the board completely.
Rhineglow is a Monk, which is excellent (resistance to ailments helps him cast).
You want him to survive the focus fire during Taunt. Maxing Defense makes every HP of that 1200 regen worth more effective health.
Lower return damage on the Destructive Core passive.
Increases the damage of the Destructive Core passive and his tile damage.
He might die before the 3 turns of Taunt are up, canceling the stored damage explosion (if dead heroes can't detonate).
. His job is to live. The damage is a bonus; the healing and protection are the win conditions.
The 1200 boosted health cannot be wiped away. Once he casts, your team
Punishes the enemy for doing exactly what they have to do (attack him).
Fixes his Slow speed vulnerability in the opening turns.
He is incredibly thick and hard to take down with tile damage alone.
Without Rush rules or mana troops, he can be too slow to save a dying team.
3 turns of Taunt goes by very fast. Smart players can just "wait it out" (ghost tiles) and then kill his team.

can be dispelled. If dispelled, enemies can ignore him and kill your squishy flanks.
At Very Fast speed, he is a nightmare. Taunt, massive heal, and damage reflection constantly.
Great for keeping the team alive on hard stages.
Viable, especially with Alpha Aether. Forces the attacker to bring a dispeller or risk a wipe.
Good luck killing anyone. Rhineglow protects the revivers while they charge.

. A 5-star Taunt hero is a cornerstone of any roster. Even at Slow speed, he will carry you through Map Stages and Raids simply by being unkillable.

. If you have those maxed and broken, Rhineglow is a "Luxury Upgrade" specifically for Rush wars or for color diversity (Nature Taunt is rare). He is worth the mats if you lack a premium Green Tank, but maybe not worth 2LB if you already have a 2LB Ludwig, unless you prioritize Rush Attack depth.
The community reaction was mixed but generally positive towards his "fun factor."
“First Law of Thermodynamics: The hero card contains all changes.”
— A physics joke from the forums.
“3 turns of taunt for slow speed alone is a pass for me.”
— A common criticism regarding his duration.
“This is what Thunderclap should have been.”
— Comparing him to the underwhelming Monster Island hero.
Most agree: In Rush Attack, he is an absolute menace.

Rhineglow is a hero who demands respect. He isn't a "fire and forget" tank like some others; he creates a mini-game within the match where the opponent has 3 turns to decide their fate. Do they hit him and risk the explosion? Do they wait and let him heal the team?
While his Slow speed limits his versatility compared to Average speed taunters, his sheer stat block and the undispellable nature of his payload make him a premier choice for specific formats.
A solid A-grade hero, rising to A+ in Rush Attack.
